findings.description: Database values display_name/action/created_at are interpolated into HTML via f-strings with no html.escape(), creating a stored XSS sink in the admin report.
The title argument is injected into the HTML template unescaped (appears in <title> and <h1>), allowing HTML/XSS injection if title is attacker-influenced.
Docstring promises an inclusive [start, end] range but the SQL uses created_at < ? which excludes the end boundary day, contradicting the stated contract.
Building the rows string via repeated += concatenation in a loop is inefficient for large result sets; ''.join() over a list is preferred.
save_report writes to a caller-supplied out_path with no validation, enabling path traversal/arbitrary file overwrite if out_path is untrusted.

Comparing a date's isoformat (YYYY-MM-DD) against created_at relies on the column being stored as a lexicographically ISO-ordered string; non-ISO or differently-typed timestamps would yield incorrect filtering.
Each query method opens and closes a fresh SQLite connection, adding overhead when methods are invoked repeatedly.
PAGE_TEMPLATE.format relies on rendered data containing no literal braces; while values are passed as args (safe), any future direct formatting of row content could raise KeyError/ValueError.
Public methods top_users and event_count lack docstrings while fetch_events and render imply a documented contract, an inconsistency in documentation coverage.
